title: sarothamnus scoparius subsp. cantabricus
intro: Sarothamnus scoparius subsp. cantabricus, also known as Cantabrian gorse or Cantabrian broom, is a subspecies of shrub in the pea family (Fabaceae) native to northern Spain and southern France. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to its high content of flavonoids and alkaloids. The health benefits of Cantabrian gorse include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as being used to treat digestive issues and respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Sarothamnus scoparius subsp. cantabricus can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
